implementation_hypotheses: a_scheduler_or_stateful_service_can_exist
minimal_resume_capability_can_exist
some_explanatory_state_can_be_exposed_to_client
peek_runtime_semantics_can_be_defined_later_without_removing_peek_from_product_surface
non_touch_surfaces_can_use_equivalent_interaction_mapping_without_forcing_literal_touch_gestures
exact_same_card_cross_device_resume_is_not_required_if_daily_progress_sync_and_some_progress_inheritance_exist
offline_use_can_be_allowed_for_authenticated_users_with_cached_state_and_content_only
sync_conflict_resolution_can_use_server_authoritative_event_merging
CloudBase physical-space canonical state uses database transactions so concurrent function instances retry read-merge-write conflicts
mobile remote authentication uses challenge-bound SMS verification, short-lived access tokens, rotating refresh tokens, and revocable server sessions
mobile refresh is demand-driven before protected requests and serialized per session so one refresh token is never submitted concurrently

mobile remote auth calls and authenticated fetch pipelines have a 15000 millisecond deadline; session replacement aborts stale requests, timeout remains retryable, and cancellation cannot become authorization rejection or mutate replacement-session state

an authenticated v2 bootstrap read derives account identity from the active session and returns membership, daily progress, learning card state, nullable scheduler cursor, physical space, and content version without accepting phone_number input
missing bootstrap components return explicit empty server state and never import device-local state; CloudBase response freshness is explicit and does not claim a cross-collection transaction
bootstrap content version is derived from normalized ordered content; production fails closed without a matching published content-release.v1 descriptor while development may expose a null release id

mobile remote login and restored sessions validate bootstrap scope before hydrating its canonical baseline, then validate the loaded card-source identity and content version before enabling learning, persisting reconciled state, replaying mutations, or pushing product-state writes

mobile keeps a still-valid authenticated session retryable after transient bootstrap failure but fails closed without previously validated canonical state and required content; reconnect reads canonical state before replay and again after replay

bootstrap plus the repository-local learning-events.v2 backend, React Native durable producer, and server scheduler do not complete global legacy snapshot-write removal, mobile scheduler-session binding, signed content manifests, production deployment, or CommonJS CloudBase production readiness

the repository-local CloudBase learning-events.v2 endpoint, React Native event client, and server scheduler implement active-session identity, immutable account-scoped events, durable event and cursor allocation before UI advance, exact replay, strict acknowledgement removal, retained content validation, transactional FSRS projection, canonical bootstrap reconciliation, server card selection, and v1 mobile learning-write removal; deployment, global v1 write removal, and mobile scheduler-session binding remain pending
development-only v1 product routes can accept an active v2 session during migration while production continues to reject every v1 route
remote content failures fail closed and never substitute bundled development cards

auth_session_store.non_sensitive_revocation_marker: versioned_AsyncStorage tombstone is written before secure credential cleanup and blocks restoration until a new secure session is saved
auth_session_store.legacy_migration: auth-session.v1 cannot be upgraded without refresh credentials and is invalidated instead of restored
auth_session_store.refresh_policy: refresh within 60 seconds of access expiry, serialize concurrent refresh demand, persist the complete rotated pair before exposing it, and never retry a rejected refresh token
auth_session_store.request_cancellation: abort an in-flight refresh when its originating session is invalidated or replaced; timeout and cancellation do not invalidate an otherwise valid session
auth_session_store.authorization_rejection: refresh rejection, repeated HTTP 401, or HTTP 403 clears account-bound persistence and returns to login

user_state_store.migration: user-state.v1 space entries receive an epoch timestamp so stale restored state cannot outrank server canonical state
user_state_store.space_state_timestamp: preserve the timestamp of each explicit favorite or sleep action

local_persistence.mutation_queue: persisted_under_an_independent_key_and_not_embedded_in_user_state; persisted mutation payloads and identifiers contain no credentials, context contains phone_number only, legacy credential-bearing membership identifiers are normalized during hydration, legacy sync_learning_state entries are discarded, serialized candidate persistence changes memory only after storage succeeds, replay results remove or retry only the exact unchanged head entry, and the current access token is injected in memory during replay
local_persistence.learning_event_outbox: learning-event-outbox.v1 is persisted under an independent AsyncStorage key; it stores immutable credential-free events, account ownership for local isolation, a pseudonymous installation ID, and the next monotonic device sequence; acknowledgements remove only matching accepted-or-duplicate events
local_persistence.logout: attempt server session revocation first, then write the local auth revocation marker and clear secure auth, user state, the generic account-bound mutation queue, and the signed-out account's learning event outbox entries before presenting logged-out runtime state; preserve the installation ID and next event sequence, and complete local cleanup when the server is unavailable
